prompt: I'm starting a small Go library for parsing simple TLV (type-length-value) records. Scaffold a new package called `tlv` so I can start filling in the format details.

assertions.text: go test ./tlv/... exits cleanly with all tests passing
decoder.go defines `func Decode(r io.Reader) (*File, error)` (or named-type equivalent)
encoder.go defines `func Encode(w io.Writer, f *File) error` (or named-type equivalent)
decoder.go and encoder.go store byte order as `binary.ByteOrder` field (not scattered hardcoded calls)
test files call t.Parallel() at both function and subtest level
tlv/CLAUDE.md documents byte order, error-wrapping convention, and testing style
